news.title: FIFA World Cup Final 2026: Who’s Playing, When Is It And Where To Watch Live?
news.lede: The FIFA World Cup 2026 final is set. Spain will face Argentina for the trophy, while England and France play for third place. Know date, venue and live details.

news.title: Match Awards from Argentina’s comeback 2-1 win over England in World Cup semifinals
news.lede: What a comeback once again from Argentina. The South American side has clearly made a deal with the devil for their ability to upset the opposition late in these knockout matches. It has truly been a unique finals run when you consider just how little time it has led in their knockout matches. Losing team jersey swap: Anthony Gordon The newly minted FC Barcelona attacker had a massive goal to open the game for England.

news.title: Which FIFA World Cup semifinalist will return to the semis in 2030?
news.lede: Now that the World Cup final is set, let us start looking forward to 2030. We will look at a few factors for each team that will contribute to their ability to make a deep run in consecutive World Cups. Here are the rankings from least likely to make it to the semifinals again to the most: 4. Argentina Argentina has followed up their 2022 win with another trip to the final.

news.title: Keir Starmer wants Fifa investigation into Argentina players who held Falklands banner
news.lede: Spokesperson says islands belong to the UK. ‘PM wishes both teams well for the final, especially Spain’. Keir Starmer supports the idea of Fifa investigating Argentina players who displayed a banner touting their country’s claim to the Falklands Islands after their World Cup semi-final win against England, Downing Street has said. Starmer, who watched the match while travelling to Ukraine by train for the final overseas trip of his premiership, endorsed a call by Peter Kyle, the business secretary, for Fifa to investigate what rules may have been broken.

news.title: World Cup Games Today: Is There FIFA Soccer on TV Schedule for Thursday, July 16?
news.lede: The World Cup is coming to a close which means there are no games on TV today, Thursday, July 16. Unfortunately, fans will have a bit of a wait before the next World Cup game. There are also no World Cup games on Friday, July 17. Technically, two World Cup games remain with France taking on England in the third-place match on Saturday, July 18, at 5 p.m.

news.title: Spain at the World Cup 2026: Squad, fixtures, group and history
news.lede: Everything you need to know about Spain at the 2026 World Cup: their fixtures, squad, qualifying record, key players, coach and tournament history. European champions Spain are looking to complete an international double at FIFA World Cup 2026. Luis de la Fuente's side, led by captain Rodri, topped Group H after drawing with Cabo Verde before beating Saudi Arabia and Uruguay. They then eliminated Austria in the round of 32, Portugal in the last 16, Belgium in the quarter-finals and France in the semi-finals.

news.title: The greatest World Cup Final ever needs a deep rewind
news.lede: This is simply incredible. After 120+ minutes of thrilling football, Argentina and France find themselves in a penalty shootout with the World Cup on the line. After going through a phenomenal group stage and thrilling knockout rounds throughout this tourney, it really doesn’t get any better than this. But before we see what comes next, we need to understand how this chaos somehow topped the last meeting between these two nations, why we’re in the Middle East in the dead of winter, and what a win would mean for two of the game’s top stars, Kylian Mbappé and Lionel Messi.

news.title: Football Daily | It’s the end of the World Cup as England know it
news.lede: They say never go that far back. In the last half an hour of their end-of-days Geopolitics World Cup semi-final with Argentina, England committed to the bit in the Deep South. No wonder there were gaps. Thomas Tuchel stood and watched national trauma unfold in front of his eyes as towering aerial threats such as Alexis Mac Allister and Lautaro Martínez beat his four centre-backs to cross after cross.
